title: coderef-watch heartbeat
description: Heartbeat written atomically by `coderef-watch` to {project-dir}/.coderef/last-scan.json on every flush attempt. LLOYD reads this on every pre-prompt assembly to compute `doc_age_seconds = now - last_scan_at`. WO-CHOKIDAR-DOC-FRESHNESS-DAEMON-001.

schema_version.description: Heartbeat schema version. Bump on breaking changes; LLOYD pins on read.

last_scan_at.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp of the most recent flush attempt (success or failure).

paths_changed.description: Workspace-relative paths in the debounce window that triggered this flush. Empty on --once or --no-pipeline modes.

status.description: pass = pipeline exited 0; fail = pipeline non-zero or spawn error; skipped = --no-pipeline flag set.

exit_code.description: Pipeline exit code, or null when no pipeline was spawned.

duration_ms.description: Wall-clock duration of the pipeline subprocess in milliseconds.

pid.description: OS PID of the coderef-watch daemon. LLOYD may probe to detect stale-but-fresh-looking heartbeats from a crashed daemon.

alive_at.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp confirming the daemon was running when this heartbeat was written. Equal to last_scan_at on a fresh write; LLOYD may treat alive_at older than N minutes as 'daemon dead' regardless of last_scan_at.

kind.description: debounce = flush triggered by chokidar event after debounce window. once = --once mode.

cwd.description: Absolute path of the workspace this daemon is watching.
